title: centaurea orphanidea subsp. thessala
intro: Centaurea orphanidea subsp. thessala, also known as the Thessalian knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to Greece. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Centaurea orphanidea subsp. thessala include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of Centaurea orphanidea subsp. thessala can be made from the plant's leaves, flowers, and roots, and are often used in traditional medicine to create teas, infusions, and tinctures.
